purpose: Traces every stage of the unified quantization pipeline for Gemma 4 E2B BF16 on vLLM to document exactly what happens and why it is infeasible on 4 GB VRAM.

stage_1_detect_precision.description: Detect whether model is full-precision or already weight-quantized
stage_1_detect_precision.explanation: Model quantization='BF16', format='safetensors' → detected as 'full_precision'. BF16 safetensors are full-precision, meaning weight quantization (Stage 2) WILL be attempted.

stage_2_size_estimates.description: Estimate VRAM footprint at each quantization level

bf16.note: 2.3B params × 2.0 bytes/param × 1024 = 4710 MB text + 7000 MB multimodal

bnb_int4.note: 2.3B params × 0.72 bytes/param × 1024 = 1695 MB text + 2450 MB multimodal (35% of BF16)

bnb_int8.note: INT8 does not reduce multimodal overhead

stage_2_size_estimates.key_insight: The multimodal encoders (SigLIP vision + audio + VQ-VAE) add 7000 MB in BF16, reduced to 2450 MB with INT4. Text-only INT4 would be just 1695 MB — small enough for 4 GB VRAM. The multimodal overhead is what makes it infeasible.

stage_3_select_quantization.description: Select best weight quantization method for available VRAM
stage_3_select_quantization.explanation: Even with INT4 quantization (4145 MB), the model exceeds the 2536 MB budget. The 2450 MB multimodal encoder overhead (35% of 7000 MB BF16) is the primary blocker.

stage_4_pipeline_decision.description: Unified quantization pipeline decision (weight quant + KV compression)
stage_4_pipeline_decision.weight_quant_reason: Model is BF16 — too large for 4096 MB VRAM even with INT4 quantization (may need smaller model or more VRAM)
stage_4_pipeline_decision.kv_reason: Applying turbo3 KV compression (4.6x, Minimal loss (+1.06% PPL))
stage_4_pipeline_decision.explanation: Pipeline detected 'full_precision' → attempted weight quantization but no method fits (Model is BF16 — too large for 4096 MB VRAM even with INT4 quantization (may need smaller model or more VRAM)). KV compression was still planned (Applying turbo3 KV compression (4.6x, Minimal loss (+1.06% PPL))) but cannot be applied if the model can't load.

stage_5_vllm_config.reason: Model too large even after INT4 quantization: BF16=11710 MB, VRAM=4096 MB
stage_5_vllm_config.explanation: build_vllm_config() returned feasible=False. Reason: Model too large even after INT4 quantization: BF16=11710 MB, VRAM=4096 MB

turboquant_kv_status.note: TurboQuant KV compression IS available on this system, but cannot be used because the model weights themselves don't fit in VRAM. KV compression only reduces the KV cache memory, not model weight memory.

minimum_hardware_analysis.description: Minimum VRAM required for Gemma 4 E2B on vLLM

your_system.verdict: RTX A2000 (4 GB) cannot run Gemma 4 E2B on vLLM because even with INT4 quantization, the multimodal encoders push the total to 4145 MB, exceeding the 2536 MB budget. Text-only INT4 (1695 MB) would fit on native Linux (2845 MB needed) but not WSL2 (3255 MB needed > 4096 MB).

minimum_hardware_analysis.recommendation: For Gemma 4 E2B on your hardware, use llama.cpp with the GGUF Q4_K_M model (2,890 MB on disk, loads with ~2.8 GB VRAM + turbo3 KV). For vLLM Gemma 4 E2B, minimum 6 GB VRAM (RTX 3060) is required.

gemma_4_e2b_BF16_INT4.blocker: Multimodal encoders add 2450 MB even with INT4
